qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Externally callable trace APIs allow attacker-controlled transaction replay resource exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about unauthenticated trace query handlers in x/vm (`TraceBlock`, `TraceTx`, `TraceCall`) exhausting resources, while the advisory is an ICS20 precompile state-consistency flaw in nested EVM execution that enables repeated use of the same token balance within one transaction.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's ICS20 precompile nested-execution state-handling bug or an equivalent source-to-sink path.
evidence: The advisory describes incorrect state handling in the ICS20 precompile during nested EVM execution, allowing the same token balance to be reused within a single transaction.
The only in-scope qualifying finding targets x/vm trace query endpoints in `grpc_query.go` and `query.pb.gw.go`, not the ICS20 precompile.
The finding's consequence is resource exhaustion from attacker-controlled tracing workloads, not token-balance reuse or nested-state inconsistency.
